abstract: Receive forwarded iOS system notifications on an accessory that you develop.
overview:
## Overview

The Accessory Notifications framework allows accessory companion apps to request notification forwarding from people, and receive notification content from the system through an extension model. People can choose to forward notifications from all apps, no apps, or a subset of apps on their device.

## Request notification forwarding

Call [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/AccessoryNotificationCenter/requestForwarding(for:)] from your companion app to prompt the person to allow notification forwarding. The system identifies your accessory through the reference you receive from [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessorySetupKit]. The method returns a [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/ForwardingDecision] that indicates the person’s choice.

## Receive and process notifications

To receive notifications, implement [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/NotificationsForwarding/AccessoryNotificationsHandler] in an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryDataProvider] extension of the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension] framework. The system calls your handler’s methods for notification arrivals, updates, or removals. Curate just the notification information that your accessory needs from the [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/AccessoryNotification] structure, which includes content for display, icons, related file attachments, and interactions.

Return the curated data to the system using the session’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryFeatureSession/sendMessage(_:)] method. The system encrypts the data using keys you provide through your app’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportSecurity] extension. Then, the system delivers the encrypted data to your app’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportAppExtension] for transmission to the accessory.

## Decrypt and display notifications

Your accessory receives the encrypted notification data and implements [https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9180/] decryption to parse the notification details. Use [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/AlertingContext] to determine whether to send an alert for the notification. The [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/AlertingContext/shouldAlert] property provides the recommended behavior that matches the system’s alerting logic.
rawMarkdown:
---
source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/accessorynotifications
crawled: 2026-03-10T14:18:24Z
---

# Accessory Notifications

**Framework**

Receive forwarded iOS system notifications on an accessory that you develop.

## Overview

The Accessory Notifications framework allows accessory companion apps to request notification forwarding from people, and receive notification content from the system through an extension model. People can choose to forward notifications from all apps, no apps, or a subset of apps on their device.



## Request notification forwarding

Call [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/AccessoryNotificationCenter/requestForwarding(for:)] from your companion app to prompt the person to allow notification forwarding. The system identifies your accessory through the reference you receive from [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessorySetupKit]. The method returns a [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/ForwardingDecision] that indicates the person’s choice.

## Receive and process notifications

To receive notifications, implement [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/NotificationsForwarding/AccessoryNotificationsHandler] in an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryDataProvider] extension of the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension] framework. The system calls your handler’s methods for notification arrivals, updates, or removals. Curate just the notification information that your accessory needs from the [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/AccessoryNotification] structure, which includes content for display, icons, related file attachments, and interactions.

Return the curated data to the system using the session’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryFeatureSession/sendMessage(_:)] method. The system encrypts the data using keys you provide through your app’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportSecurity] extension. Then, the system delivers the encrypted data to your app’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AccessoryTransportExtension/AccessoryTransportAppExtension] for transmission to the accessory.

## Decrypt and display notifications

Your accessory receives the encrypted notification data and implements [https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9180/] decryption to parse the notification details. Use [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/AlertingContext] to determine whether to send an alert for the notification. The [doc://com.apple.accessorynotifications/documentation/AccessoryNotifications/AlertingContext/shouldAlert] property provides the recommended behavior that matches the system’s alerting logic.



## Essentials

- **Receiving iOS notifications on an accessory**: Create custom app extensions that manage notifications for your accessory.

## Authorization

- **AccessoryNotificationCenter**: A class that enables an app to request permission for notification forwarding.
- **ForwardingDecision**: Possible decisions in response to the notification forwarding permission prompt.

## Notification receipt

- **NotificationsForwarding**: A class for handling notification forwarding in your accessory’s data provider extension.
- **NotificationsForwarding.AccessoryNotificationsHandler**: A protocol that defines methods for handling notification lifecycle events in your extension.
- **NotificationsForwarding.Session**: A session object that enables communication between your extension and the system.
- **NotificationsForwarding.HandlerFactory**: A type alias for a factory that creates notification handlers.

## Data curation and alerting

- **AccessoryNotification**: A structure that contains the details of a notification that iOS provides to your accessory.
- **AlertingContext**: A structure that provides guidance for how to alert for a notification.
- **AlertCoordinating**: A protocol that communicates whether the accessory completes the process of alerting for a notification.

## Interactive support

- **AccessoryNotificationManaging**: A protocol that enables the communication of notification responses to the system.
- **AccessoryNotificationManagerFactory**: A factory class that provides access to the system notification manager.
- **NotificationResponse**: A person’s response to a notification.

## Errors

- **AccessoryError**: Errors the Accessory Notifications framework can throw.
